astrocade-qa-calibration-tool.title: Astrocade AI QA Calibration Tool
astrocade-qa-calibration-tool.description: Designed and operated a human-in-the-loop QA calibration system for Astrocade's AI-powered UGC pipeline, connecting auto-review, QA annotation, final review, creator feedback, and operational guardrails to improve decision consistency and daily publishing throughput.

details.proofRole: human-in-the-loop AI systems for creator-facing moderation

situation.title: Overview
situation.description: Astrocade needed fast publishing decisions for an AI-powered UGC platform without treating auto-review as final authority. A one-day review target required clear, repeatable handoffs between automation and human judgment.

situation.title: Quality risk
situation.description: False, repeated, or inconsistent rejections created friction for creators and slowed publishing. Disagreement between automated decisions and reviewers had to become visible, explainable, and actionable.

situation.title: Operating loop
situation.description: The workflow connected auto-review, QA annotation, calibration, final review, creator feedback, and policy updates. Human judgment served as an accountable control point, not a late-stage exception.

task.title: Design the operating model
task.description: Create a QA calibration system that turns model-assisted and human review into one continuous decision loop, from initial flag through final review and creator-facing feedback.

task.title: Make quality measurable
task.description: Define reviewable signals for agreement rate, repeat or reversed rejections, and turnaround time so calibration priorities could be based on observed operational behavior.

task.title: Protect creator outcomes
task.description: Establish final-review behavior, escalation paths, and feedback standards that preserve safety while giving creators a more consistent path to publishing.

actions.title: Built QA annotation workflows
actions.description: Created repeatable QA passes that compare auto-review and reviewer decisions, annotate disagreement and edge-case patterns, and turn those patterns into focused follow-up work.

actions.title: Ran calibration and policy loops
actions.description: Led recurring audits that paired sampled decisions with policy intent, then translated findings into threshold, tooling, documentation, and reviewer-guidance updates.

actions.title: Defined final-review guardrails
actions.description: Clarified when final reviewers could confirm, override, or escalate a decision so human intervention was consistent, accountable, and operationally legible.

actions.title: Closed creator feedback loops
actions.description: Connected rejection feedback and recurring creator issues back to calibration, documentation, and workflow fixes instead of treating each review outcome as an isolated event.

results.title: A measurable quality-control loop
results.description: Made reviewer-model alignment, repeat or reversed rejections, and queue turnaround concrete operating signals for ongoing calibration and prioritization.

results.title: More consistent decisions
results.description: Recurring QA annotation and calibration improved alignment between automated decisions, final reviewers, and policy intent across the moderation pipeline.

results.title: Faster, more trustworthy creator publishing
results.description: Clear handoffs, operational guardrails, and creator feedback loops supported reliable day-level review targets while reducing avoidable review friction.

wizzo.title: Wizzo
wizzo.description: Designed and built an AI mentor product system that turns chat, connected work context, goals, and follow-up into actionable quests.

situation.title: Intent-to-Action Gap
situation.description: AI conversations often stop at advice. Wizzo reframes that moment as an execution problem: how to turn goals, blockers, deadlines, and recent progress into next steps a user can actually finish.

situation.title: Product Context
situation.description: The public beta positions Wizzo as an AI adventure mentor that combines chat, voice, connected work context, achievement loops, and quest-based follow-through.

situation.title: System Context
situation.description: The product needed to connect AI guidance with real work surfaces while keeping privacy, account controls, and product trust visible from the start.

task.title: Design an AI Mentor Loop
task.description: Create a product loop where chat can capture intent, remember execution context, suggest grounded next steps, and translate progress into quests.

task.title: Connect Work Context
task.description: Support Drive, Gmail, Calendar, and technical workflows so the AI mentor can reason from the artifacts and deadlines that shape real follow-through.

task.title: Make AI Actions Grounded
task.description: Pair conversation with tools for search, research, code execution, image generation, files, and voice so the assistant can move beyond generic coaching.

task.title: Preserve User Control
task.description: Build account export, confirmed deletion, and privacy-aware controls into the product system so personal progress data remains manageable.

actions.title: Product System Architecture
actions.description: Designed the core experience around main chat, execution-aware memory, quest tracking, achievements, and connector-backed work context.

actions.title: Full-Stack Implementation
actions.description: Built the product with Next.js, TypeScript, Postgres, Drizzle ORM, Neon, Vercel, OpenAI integrations, and Google workspace connectors.

actions.title: AI Workflow Surfaces
actions.description: Created mentor interactions for goal planning, progress reflection, grounded research, connected notes, and voice-supported follow-up.

actions.title: Trust and Account Controls
actions.description: Added export and confirmed deletion paths covering product, AI, notification, integration, ML-derived, and community records.

results.title: Public Beta
results.description: Shipped a public beta with a marketing site, live web app, AI mentor interface, quest system, and connected work context.

results.title: AI Product Proof
results.description: Demonstrated an AI-native product system that combines chat, memory, work connectors, tools, privacy controls, and habit-forming progress loops.

results.title: Portfolio Relevance
results.description: Serves as proof of product design, engineering execution, AI workflow design, and operational thinking across a real shipped SaaS surface.

x-games.title: X Games
x-games.description: Built an AI-first social game platform that turns any X post into an instant browser game, using Grok-powered concept generation, playable game cards, social sharing, and leaderboards to make AI game creation feel native to X.

situation.title: Overview
situation.description: X Games explores a compact AI-native loop: turn social posts from X into playable browser games that can be discovered, shared, and ranked.

situation.title: Challenge
situation.description: The product needed to make AI game generation feel immediate and understandable while still supporting recognizable game cards, trending discovery, and leaderboard behavior.

situation.title: Context
situation.description: The experience sits at the intersection of AI-assisted creation, social distribution, and lightweight game UX, with Grok positioned as the generation engine.

task.title: AI-First Product Loop
task.description: Design a clear path from an X post to an instant playable game, keeping the AI promise visible in the hero, game cards, and calls to action.

task.title: Discovery and Ranking
task.description: Support a browsing surface where generated games can trend, accumulate engagement, and move users toward play.

task.title: Shareable Game UX
task.description: Make each generated game feel like a social object that can be played, shared, and evaluated quickly.

actions.title: Built AI-forward positioning
actions.description: Centered the interface around the promise that any X post can become an instant game, with repeated Grok-powered AI signals throughout the experience.

actions.title: Designed game-card discovery
actions.description: Created a trending game surface with genre labels, generated thumbnails, creator attribution, play counts, and direct play actions.

actions.title: Connected social mechanics
actions.description: Integrated sign-in, sharing, and leaderboard affordances so AI-generated games feel connected to the X ecosystem.

results.title: AI-first portfolio proof
results.description: Adds a visible example of AI-assisted product design and game generation to the portfolio homepage and project archive.

results.title: Fast concept-to-play loop
results.description: Shows how AI can compress ideation, generation, discovery, and play into a lightweight browser experience.

results.title: Social game platform direction
results.description: Establishes a foundation for AI-generated games with trending discovery, shareability, and ranking behavior.

gaia.title: Gaia
gaia.description: Turned Starbucks store data into spatial operations and training workflows, connecting analytics, prototyping, and enterprise internal-tool UX.

actions.title: User Research
actions.description: Conducted research to understand the needs of Starbucks employees and customers in a spatial computing context.

actions.title: Design
actions.description: Designed wireframes and prototypes using Figma, focusing on intuitive and accessible interfaces.

actions.title: Development
actions.description: Developed spatial computing prototypes using Unity3D, ensuring seamless integration with Starbucks' systems.

actions.title: Testing
actions.description: Conducted usability testing with employees and customers to refine the applications.

actions.title: Collaboration
actions.description: Worked with cross-functional teams to align digital solutions with operational needs.

details.result: The Gaia project successfully enhanced operational efficiency and customer experience, setting new benchmarks in retail technology. My contributions in design and development created a more engaging, intuitive, and immersive retail environment, advancing Starbucks' vision for a digitally integrated future.

apt-plus.title: APT+
apt-plus.description: Ford APT+ is an end-to-end solution focused on streamlining manufacturing workflows by improving the accuracy and efficiency of time studies. These studies, previously slow and error-prone, cost Ford millions of dollars annually in wasted effort. My goal with APT+ was to design and implement a precision-driven, scalable system that eliminated inefficiencies and enabled data-driven decision-making - ultimately saving Ford approximately $1M per plant each year.

situation.title: Overview
situation.description: Ford's manufacturing processes heavily rely on time studies to identify bottlenecks and optimize production lines. However, existing approaches were:

situation.title: Labor-Intensive
situation.description: Manual tracking led to frequent errors.

situation.title: Slow and Costly
situation.description: Collecting and interpreting data took too long, directly impacting production efficiency.

situation.title: Data Silos
situation.description: Lack of integrated systems made it difficult to draw insights from collected information.

task.title: Overview
task.description: The objective was clear: build an intelligent, user-centric platform to conduct time studies more efficiently and accurately. Key goals included:

task.title: Reduce Manual Errors
task.description: through automated data collection and analysis.

task.title: Enhance Data Transparency
task.description: by centralizing results in a scalable system.

task.title: Enable Data-Driven Decision-Making
task.description: so stakeholders could act on real-time insights.

actions.title: Overview
actions.description: I led the design and engineering strategy, collaborating with project managers, software engineers, and researchers to:

actions.title: Identify Bottlenecks
actions.description: Conducted field research to pinpoint the most time-consuming or error-prone tasks in existing processes.

actions.title: Rapid Prototyping & Iteration
actions.description: Created proof-of-concept solutions in close coordination with stakeholders, then refined these prototypes based on real-world feedback.

actions.title: Automation & Accuracy
actions.description: Integrated data capture tools and algorithms to reduce human error, streamline data entry, and highlight critical insights for faster decision-making.

actions.title: Cross-Functional Alignment
actions.description: Worked with engineering, design, and business units to ensure the final solution met broader operational goals, including cost reduction and user adoption.

results.title: Saved Approximately $1M/Year per Plant
results.description: By reducing manual errors and expediting time studies, each facility saw significant cost savings.

results.title: Increased Efficiency & Accuracy
results.description: Automated workflows cut the overall time spent on data collection and validation, boosting productivity on the assembly line.

results.title: Provided Scalable Insights
results.description: A centralized data platform allowed decision-makers to track performance metrics in real time and roll out process improvements across multiple plants.

transcribe.title: Transcribe
transcribe.description: Starbucks sought to improve communication in their stores, particularly in drive-through scenarios, by implementing real-time speech-to-text transcription. This initiative aimed to enhance inclusivity and operational efficiency. As the Product UX/UI Designer and React Developer, I designed and developed the Transcribe application to address unique communication challenges in high-traffic retail environments.

details.situation: Starbucks sought to improve communication in their stores, particularly in drive-through scenarios, by implementing real-time speech-to-text transcription. This initiative aimed to enhance inclusivity and operational efficiency.
details.task: As the Product UX/UI Designer and React Developer, my responsibility was to design and develop the Transcribe application. The goal was to address unique communication challenges in high-traffic retail environments and ensure the tool was effective and user-friendly.

actions.title: User Research
actions.description: Conducted user research to understand the needs of Starbucks employees and customers, especially focusing on individuals with disabilities.

actions.title: Design
actions.description: Created wireframes and prototypes using Figma, ensuring the interface was intuitive and accessible.

actions.title: Development
actions.description: Developed the application using React.js and integrated it with Google Cloud's Speech-to-Text API for real-time transcription.

actions.title: Testing
actions.description: Conducted rigorous testing, including usability testing with individuals with disabilities, to ensure the tool met diverse needs.

actions.title: Integration
actions.description: Integrated the application into Starbucks' existing operational framework, ensuring seamless deployment.

details.result: The Transcribe initiative significantly enhanced customer satisfaction and operational fluidity. It improved transcription accuracy by 30% and reduced manual note-taking time by 50%. This project showcased my expertise in UX/UI design and development and emphasized my commitment to creating accessible and inclusive digital solutions.

geovoice.title: GeoVoice
geovoice.description: GeoVoice is a platform created to streamline geospatial data analysis and stakeholder feedback, primarily for large-scale infrastructure and environmental planning projects. By integrating intuitive mapping tools with a centralized feedback system, GeoVoice enables teams to gather, visualize, and act on geographically specific insights in real time. The result is a more transparent and efficient way to collaborate, whether for urban development, energy planning, or environmental impact assessments.

situation.title: Overview
situation.description: Large-scale projects often involve multiple stakeholders - community members, government agencies, and private sector partners - who need to provide location-specific feedback. Existing processes (e.g., in-person forums, paper surveys) can be slow, fragmented, and prone to data loss.

situation.title: Challenge
situation.description: How might we simplify the process of collecting and analyzing spatially relevant feedback to make more informed decisions?

situation.title: Context
situation.description: Advances in geospatial technology offered new possibilities for data visualization, but most tools weren't user-friendly or collaborative enough for diverse, non-technical audiences.

task.title: Consolidates
task.description: multiple map layers and real-time data inputs.

task.title: Facilitates
task.description: stakeholder engagement through an easy-to-use interface for comments and feedback.

task.title: Empowers
task.description: project teams to make quicker, more accurate decisions using interactive mapping and clear data visualization.

actions.title: Research & Alignment
actions.description: Conducted sessions with local planners, environmental experts, and community members to pinpoint their biggest pain points in collecting and sharing geographic feedback. Evaluated existing GIS tools and collaboration platforms, identifying gaps in usability and real-time interaction.

actions.title: Rapid Prototyping
actions.description: Created low-fidelity sketches and mid-fidelity prototypes focusing on map layouts, layered data displays, and straightforward feedback mechanisms. Iteratively tested prototypes with target audiences, refining the interface to reduce complexity and ensure clarity.

actions.title: Development & Integration
actions.description: Incorporated multiple map layers (e.g., demographics, zoning, environmental impact) to provide context for each feedback submission. Built a central dashboard where stakeholders could track updates, leave comments, and view geospatial analytics. Ensured the platform could handle large datasets and secure sensitive user information, particularly important for municipal or corporate clients.

results.title: Enhanced Collaboration
results.description: A centralized environment allowed diverse stakeholders to contribute geospatial insights, reducing silos.

results.title: Faster Feedback Loops
results.description: Real-time data updates and straightforward comment submissions cut weeks off traditional planning cycles.

results.title: Broader Applicability
results.description: The modular design supported expansion across multiple regions and project types, from infrastructure upgrades to environmental monitoring.

speakeasy.title: SpeakEasy
speakeasy.description: Designed a voice-controlled mixed reality accessibility system for users with low muscle tone, using AI-assisted interaction patterns to reduce physical input demands in XR environments.

details.client: Master of Design in Experience Design, SJSU

situation.title: Overview
situation.description: Extended Reality holds transformative promise but often remains a domain accessible only to the tech-savvy. Current XR systems typically require precise physical inputs and complex navigation, thereby excluding users with disabilities and limited motor control.

situation.title: Challenge
situation.description: Users with low muscle tone struggle with conventional XR interfaces that depend on manual interaction and intricate gesture controls.

situation.title: Context
situation.description: Despite rapid advancements in XR hardware and software, many experiences lack the simplicity and naturalness required for universal accessibility.

task.title: Overview
task.description: The core objective was to design an XR experience that minimizes physical strain while maximizing intuitive interaction. I set out to develop a voice-driven AI system - SpeakEasy - that would:

task.title: Reduce physical interaction
task.description: by shifting control to natural language commands.

task.title: Personalize the user experience
task.description: using adaptive AI to respond to individual needs.

task.title: Facilitate inclusive design
task.description: that benefits users across a wide spectrum of abilities.

actions.title: Research & Literature Review
actions.description: I conducted a comprehensive review of XR accessibility challenges, integrating insights from academic sources, industry trends, and competitive analyses.

actions.title: Co-Design Workshops
actions.description: Engaging with target users and experts, I organized iterative workshops that enabled real-time feedback and guided the evolution of the interface design.

actions.title: Prototype Development
actions.description: Beginning with early sketches and wireframes in ShapesXR, I rapidly moved through iterative cycles - transitioning to a Unity3D prototype - to incorporate voice recognition and multimodal feedback mechanisms.

actions.title: Feedback & Iteration
actions.description: Continuous testing, supported by participant recruitment and structured field protocols, allowed for refinements in interaction logic and overall system adaptability.

results.title: Overview
results.description: The iterative process has yielded a promising framework that demonstrates:

results.title: Enhanced Accessibility
results.description: Early user tests indicate a marked reduction in physical interaction requirements, allowing individuals with low muscle tone to navigate XR environments more comfortably.

results.title: Improved User Engagement
results.description: Participants reported a more natural and engaging experience, with voice commands enabling quicker, more intuitive interactions compared to traditional input methods.

results.title: A Scalable Framework
results.description: The success of SpeakEasy's initial iterations lays the groundwork for broader applications in XR, supporting further research into complementary modalities like gesture recognition.

exhibition.title: Interactive Stations
exhibition.description: Live demonstrations of the prototype where visitors can experience the voice-driven interface firsthand.

exhibition.title: User Testimonials
exhibition.description: Video recordings and written feedback from participants will highlight the impact of the design on accessibility and usability.

exhibition.title: Future Concepts
exhibition.description: Insights into planned enhancements, including advanced gesture recognition and deeper personalization features.

petition-ready.title: PetitionReady
petition-ready.description: Built an AI-assisted bankruptcy intake readiness console for paralegals, focused on case blockers, document gaps, readiness scoring, client follow-up, and attorney-review packet preparation without crossing into legal advice.

situation.title: Overview
situation.description: Bankruptcy firms lose time when intake packets are incomplete, income data conflicts, creditor addresses are missing, documents are rejected, or payment status is unclear. Those exceptions often surface late, after a paralegal has already started assembling a packet for attorney review.

situation.title: Challenge
situation.description: The product needed to answer one practical workflow question: can this client matter move toward attorney review, and if not, what exact blocker should the paralegal resolve next?

situation.title: Guardrail
situation.description: The prototype intentionally focuses on readiness, blockers, evidence, and attorney-review preparation instead of automated filing, legal advice, eligibility decisions, or petition generation.

task.title: Readiness Console
task.description: Design and build a working dashboard that gives paralegals a clear view of active matters, blocker counts, readiness scores, queue status, and next actions.

task.title: Case-Grounded AI
task.description: Use structured AI outputs to summarize blockers, find conflicts, draft client follow-ups, and compile attorney-review briefs from the current case packet.

task.title: Reviewer-Safe Prototype
task.description: Make the demo useful without credentials through deterministic local fallback analysis, while preserving the same UI shape when model calls are unavailable.

actions.title: Modeled the bankruptcy intake domain
actions.description: Created seeded Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 matters with client facts, document states, retainer status, issues, readiness stages, raw notes, and blocker metadata.

actions.title: Built the operational dashboard
actions.description: Implemented readiness summary cards, matter queue pagination, status badges, selected matter details, top blockers, next paralegal action, and attorney-review item surfaces.

actions.title: Implemented AI-assisted review flows
actions.description: Built Paralegal Copilot prompt modes for blocker analysis, conflict checks, client follow-up drafts, and attorney-review brief compilation with structured JSON validation.

actions.title: Added deterministic fallback behavior
actions.description: Created local analysis for readiness scoring, blocker detection, follow-up generation, and brief assembly so reviewers can evaluate the full workflow without an OpenAI key.

results.title: Clear human-in-the-loop AI boundary
results.description: PetitionReady keeps the paralegal and attorney review loop visible by grounding outputs in supplied case evidence and avoiding legal conclusions.

results.title: Operational UX proof
results.description: The interface turns messy intake state into a queue, score, issue list, evidence snapshot, and next action that a paralegal can operate repeatedly.

results.title: Production-minded prototype
results.description: The repo includes typed domain models, structured AI response validation, focused Vitest coverage, and a Vercel demo that works with or without model credentials.

creative-supply-engine.title: Creative Supply Engine
creative-supply-engine.description: Built a Python creative automation pipeline that turns structured campaign briefs and reusable product assets into localized AI-assisted social creatives across 1:1, 9:16, and 16:9 formats, with deterministic logo/text overlays, compliance checks, run logs, and review galleries.

situation.title: Overview
situation.description: Campaign teams need to produce consistent, localized social creative across multiple formats without rebuilding every asset by hand.

situation.title: Challenge
situation.description: AI image generation can help create campaign hero assets, but brand, copy, compliance, sizing, and review requirements still need deterministic controls.

situation.title: Context
situation.description: The sample Pulse Beverages campaign uses reusable product assets, structured YAML briefs, localized market copy, and required logo/compliance rules.

task.title: Reusable Creative Pipeline
task.description: Create a CLI workflow that can read a campaign brief, reuse existing hero assets when available, generate missing assets when configured, and output production-ready social ratios.

task.title: Localized Output System
task.description: Support multiple products and locales while preserving brand rules, CTA text, campaign messages, and safe layout behavior across each ratio.

task.title: Review and Compliance
task.description: Produce reviewable outputs, run logs, and compliance checks so generated creative can be inspected before campaign use.

actions.title: Built the CLI
actions.description: Packaged the workflow as the pulse-cse Python command with structured brief loading, campaign logging, and repeatable output paths.

actions.title: Created reusable asset logic
actions.description: Implemented local asset reuse first, with an OpenAI image generation path and graceful placeholder fallback when live generation is unavailable.

actions.title: Generated responsive creative sets
actions.description: Used Pillow to crop and compose 1:1, 9:16, and 16:9 outputs with deterministic logo placement, localized text overlays, and brand color treatment.

actions.title: Added review outputs
actions.description: Produced run logs, compliance summaries, and a browser-friendly review gallery for checking localized creative sets quickly.

results.title: Twelve creative outputs
results.description: Generated a sample Summer Citrus Reset campaign covering two products, two locales, and three social ratios per locale.

results.title: Passed compliance checks
results.description: The run confirmed expected dimensions, required logo application, message presence, and prohibited-word checks across the generated assets.

results.title: AI workflow proof
results.description: Shows a practical AI-assisted creative operations system where generation, localization, deterministic layout, and human review work together.

material-explorer.title: Material Explorer
material-explorer.description: Material Explorer is a production-minded interactive web app for creating, comparing, and sharing physically based 3D materials in real time. Built with React + Three.js/React Three Fiber, it now includes autosave/restore, A/B compare, texture workflows, draft history, and shareable presets for rapid iteration.

situation.title: Overview
situation.description: 3D material creation and customization typically requires specialized knowledge and software, creating barriers for designers and developers who want to experiment with materials for their web-based 3D projects. Existing tools often lack real-time feedback or are too complex for quick iterations.

situation.title: Challenge
situation.description: How might we simplify the process of creating and customizing 3D materials for the web while providing immediate visual feedback in an intuitive interface?

situation.title: Context
situation.description: With the growing popularity of 3D on the web through libraries like Three.js and React Three Fiber, there's an increasing need for accessible tools that bridge the gap between technical implementation and visual design.

task.title: Overview
task.description: The goal was to create a user-friendly web application that makes 3D material creation and customization accessible to both developers and designers. The application needed to:

task.title: Simplify Material Creation
task.description: by providing an intuitive interface for adjusting material properties without requiring deep technical knowledge.

task.title: Provide Real-time Feedback
task.description: through immediate visual updates as users modify material properties.

task.title: Ensure Cross-device Compatibility
task.description: by creating a responsive design that works well on various devices and screen sizes.

actions.title: Built a full PBR material lab
actions.description: Implemented real-time controls for color, metalness, roughness, emissive, clearcoat, transmission/IOR, opacity, and texture-map channels with instant preview updates.

actions.title: Shipped rapid-iteration workflows
actions.description: Added draft autosave/restore, undo/redo history, compare mode, duplicate/randomize utilities, and draft JSON import/export to speed creative exploration.

actions.title: Improved sharing and portability
actions.description: Added shareable URLs for material state, JSON import/export, and GLB export options for better collaboration and reuse.

actions.title: Hardened quality for ongoing delivery
actions.description: Set up CI quality gates (format, lint, type-check, tests, build, bundle checks, Playwright e2e) and tightened release/README hygiene for maintainability.

results.title: Faster iteration loops
results.description: Reduced friction for testing material ideas by combining real-time preview with autosave, history, and one-click power tools.

results.title: Portfolio-ready engineering signal
results.description: Demonstrates modern React/TypeScript architecture, 3D interaction design, and disciplined shipping practices in an actively maintained repo.

results.title: Better project communication
results.description: Live demo + GitHub source + structured project narrative make the work easier for recruiters and hiring teams to evaluate quickly.

vulnerability-visualizer.title: Vulnerability Visualizer
vulnerability-visualizer.description: Built a React + TypeScript security dashboard for analyzing large vulnerability datasets, with AI analysis modes, manual vs AI triage comparisons, severity filtering, virtualized lists, comparison workflows, and export tools for security review.

situation.title: Overview
situation.description: Security review teams need to inspect large vulnerability datasets quickly while separating urgent issues from low-risk noise.

situation.title: Challenge
situation.description: The interface had to make hundreds of thousands of vulnerability records searchable, filterable, comparable, and readable without overwhelming reviewers.

situation.title: Context
situation.description: The dataset includes manual and AI-assisted status signals, severity levels, package metadata, risk factors, and export needs across review workflows.

task.title: Dashboard Workflow
task.description: Create a dashboard that turns raw vulnerability data into a usable review surface for filtering, analysis, comparison, and export.

task.title: AI Analysis Visibility
task.description: Make AI-assisted triage behavior visible through analysis modes, manual vs AI comparison charts, and clear status filtering.

task.title: Large Dataset Performance
task.description: Keep the interface responsive while rendering and searching large datasets with virtualized lists and optimized state handling.

actions.title: Built interactive filtering
actions.description: Implemented severity, status, search, and analysis-mode controls that let reviewers narrow large vulnerability datasets quickly.

actions.title: Visualized AI and manual triage
actions.description: Added charts for severity distribution, risk factor frequency, and manual vs AI assessment relationships to make review patterns easier to inspect.

actions.title: Supported comparison workflows
actions.description: Created a compare flow that lets reviewers select multiple vulnerabilities, inspect them side by side, and preserve context while moving through the dataset.

actions.title: Hardened the data experience
actions.description: Used React Query, persisted preferences, export utilities, resilient data loading, and virtualized rendering to keep the app usable at production-scale data volume.

results.title: Security review at scale
results.description: The dashboard can load and analyze hundreds of thousands of vulnerability records while keeping filters, charts, and review controls usable.

results.title: Clear AI workflow signal
results.description: The project demonstrates how AI-assisted security triage can be exposed through practical reviewer-facing UI instead of hidden automation.

results.title: Recruiter-friendly proof
results.description: The live demo and GitHub repo show hands-on frontend engineering across TypeScript, data visualization, performance, and operational security workflows.

sound-escape-vr.title: Sound Escape VR
sound-escape-vr.description: Built a VR music creation prototype that combines a grid-based sequencer, real-time audio visualization, and responsive world-building to make spatial composition feel approachable.

situation.title: Overview
situation.description: Virtual reality offers unique opportunities for music creation and visualization, but many existing applications either focus solely on professional music production or provide only passive music visualization experiences. There was a gap in the market for accessible, intuitive music creation tools that leverage the immersive potential of VR.

situation.title: Challenge
situation.description: How might we create an engaging VR experience that allows users with varying levels of musical expertise to create and visualize music in an intuitive, embodied way?

situation.title: Context
situation.description: The rise of consumer VR headsets like Meta Quest 3 has created new opportunities for innovative music applications that go beyond traditional interfaces.

task.title: Overview
task.description: The goal was to develop an immersive VR application that combines music creation with visual feedback in a cohesive, engaging experience. The project needed to:

task.title: Simplify Music Creation
task.description: by implementing an intuitive grid-based sequencer that doesn't require musical expertise.

task.title: Enhance Immersion
task.description: through real-time audio visualization that responds dynamically to user-created sounds.

task.title: Optimize Performance
task.description: to ensure smooth operation on standalone VR headsets like the Meta Quest 3.

actions.title: Technical Architecture
actions.description: Developed the application using Unity3D with C# as the primary programming language. Implemented custom shaders for responsive visual effects and optimized audio processing for real-time feedback.

actions.title: User Interface Design
actions.description: Created an intuitive spatial interface that leverages natural hand movements for music composition, allowing users to place notes on a three-dimensional grid sequencer.

actions.title: Audio-Visual Integration
actions.description: Programmed a system that translates musical parameters (pitch, volume, rhythm) into visual elements that transform the environment, creating a synesthetic experience.

actions.title: Performance Optimization
actions.description: Reworked the original Oculus implementation to ensure optimal performance on Meta Quest 3, focusing on maintaining high frame rates while preserving visual quality.

results.title: Overview
results.description: Sound Escape VR successfully bridges the gap between music creation and visualization in virtual reality:

results.title: Intuitive Music Creation
results.description: Users can quickly learn to create musical patterns using the grid-based sequencer, regardless of their prior musical experience.

results.title: Immersive Audio-Visual Experience
results.description: The environment reacts and transforms in sync with user-created music, creating a deeply engaging experience that connects sound and visuals.

results.title: Technical Achievement
results.description: Successfully optimized for Meta Quest 3, demonstrating the ability to create complex, interactive experiences for standalone VR headsets.

portals.title: Portals
portals.description: Built an accessible Snap Spectacles AR prototype that uses spatial audio and computer vision to connect music, culture, collaboration, and climate awareness.

details.client: Stanford's Immerse the Bay 2024 XR Hackathon

situation.title: Overview
situation.description: As AR technologies become more widespread, there's a growing need for experiences that prioritize accessibility, cultural education, and climate awareness. Many existing AR applications focus primarily on visual elements, often overlooking audio-based interactions and accessibility features.

situation.title: Challenge
situation.description: How might we create an immersive AR experience that makes diverse musical traditions accessible to everyone, including individuals with limited mobility, while promoting cultural education and climate awareness?

situation.title: Context
situation.description: Snap Spectacles provide a unique platform for AR development with spatial audio capabilities, offering new possibilities for creating inclusive and educational experiences.

task.title: Overview
task.description: The goal was to develop an AR experience for Snap Spectacles that would achieve four main objectives:

task.title: Accessibility
task.description: Design inclusive, voice-controlled features and audio cues for individuals with limited hand mobility or speech difficulties.

task.title: Cultural Education
task.description: Enable auditory exploration of music from various cultures, focusing on historical context and lesser-known instruments.

task.title: Joy
task.description: Create fun, collaborative AR jam sessions with friends using spatial audio.

task.title: Climate Change Awareness
task.description: Highlight music that connects to natural environments or climate themes to increase awareness through audio immersion.

actions.title: Co-Creation Features
actions.description: Developed spatial audio circles that position friends and collaborators in a virtual jam session, each playing an instrument or adding to the ambiance, allowing friends to interact audibly to avoid visual clutter.

actions.title: Interactive Auditory Timeline
actions.description: Implemented cultural markers recognition using Spectacles' computer vision to identify cultural artifacts or historical sites and launch a timeline with music, stories, and significant figures from that culture.

actions.title: Accessibility Implementation
actions.description: Created voice command controls for the experience, providing accessibility for individuals with limited mobility, along with a custom 'safe word' exit feature to prioritize user comfort and safety.

actions.title: Educational Layers
actions.description: Integrated optional deep dives into the history and significance of various music elements for educational purposes.

results.title: Inclusive AR Experience
results.description: Successfully created an AR experience that prioritizes accessibility through voice controls and audio-based interactions.

results.title: Cultural Education Platform
results.description: Developed a platform for exploring diverse musical traditions and their historical contexts, focusing on underrepresented communities.

results.title: Collaborative Music Creation
results.description: Implemented spatial audio features that enable users to create collaborative, immersive musical experiences with friends.

results.title: Climate Awareness Integration
results.description: Incorporated elements that highlight the connection between music, natural environments, and climate themes to raise awareness through immersive audio experiences.

die-ai.title: Die, AI!
die-ai.description: A Flash shooter demo built in Adobe Animate and ActionScript, now revived for modern browsers with Ruffle.

details.situation: Hackers reprogrammed AICorp's Buddy Bots to break out and destroy humanity. Dr. Gimmstein must stop them with his prototype laser gun.
details.task: Create a simple Flash game inspired by a shoot-em-up template and enhance it with levels, intro screens, and audio.

actions.title: Custom ActionScript Mechanics
actions.description: Extended a Shoot game template with health bars, lasers, enemy AI, and Greensock transitions.

actions.title: Ruffle Revival
actions.description: Repackaged the original SWF with an HTML loader using the Ruffle WebAssembly emulator.

details.result: A playable Flash demo preserved for the modern web.

ai-energy-consumption.title: AI Energy Consumption
ai-energy-consumption.description: Built an interactive 3D data visualization that explains AI energy consumption and CO2 emissions across countries and regions using A-Frame, D3.js, and clear data storytelling.

situation.title: Overview
situation.description: As AI technologies continue to advance and become more widespread, their energy consumption and environmental impact are growing concerns that often go unnoticed by the general public and even many technology professionals.

situation.title: Challenge
situation.description: Presenting complex environmental data in an engaging, accessible way is difficult. Traditional charts and graphs often fail to convey the scale and significance of the issue in a way that resonates with viewers.

situation.title: Context
situation.description: The rise of AI models with billions of parameters has led to exponential growth in computing requirements, with corresponding increases in energy consumption and carbon emissions.

task.title: Overview
task.description: The goal was to create an interactive 3D visualization that makes abstract data about AI energy consumption tangible and impactful for users.

task.title: Educational Objective
task.description: Raise awareness about the environmental impact of AI technologies in an engaging, interactive format.

task.title: Technical Objective
task.description: Combine web-based 3D visualization with accurate data representation to create an immersive learning experience.

actions.title: Research & Data Collection
actions.description: Gathered and analyzed data on AI energy consumption across different countries and regions, focusing on both training and inference phases of AI systems.

actions.title: Technical Implementation
actions.description: Developed a web-based 3D visualization using A-Frame for the immersive environment and D3.js for data binding and manipulation. Created custom shaders and animations to represent energy consumption patterns.

actions.title: User Experience Design
actions.description: Designed an intuitive interface that allows users to explore the data from different perspectives, with interactive elements that reveal additional information and context.

results.title: Engaging Visualization
results.description: Created a compelling 3D experience that transforms abstract data into a visually striking representation of AI's environmental impact.

results.title: Educational Tool
results.description: The project serves as both a data visualization and an educational resource, helping users understand the environmental implications of AI advancement.

results.title: Technical Achievement
results.description: Successfully integrated complex 3D graphics with data visualization techniques in a web-based application that performs well across different devices.
